gloss:
A cover lets every pairwise-consistent, locally-nonempty family of local relations glue into a single global object exactly when the cover is acyclic. This equivalence is proved in a separate library (a discrete version of Vorob'ev's gluing theorem), and it is what separates the covers where local data determines the global object from those where it cannot.

**Cites** `HiddenChannelCapacity.acyclic_iff_forall_consistent_glues` — the corpus theorem this result restates and discharges.

**Original contribution.** The corpus supplies the acyclic-versus-cyclic equivalence as a combinatorial fact; the thesis-layer statement positions it as the external, independent source of the theory's non-triviality, showing the dividing line comes from an outside library rather than any choice made inside the theory.

statement.pretty: ∀ {ι : Type u_1} [inst : DecidableEq ι] [inst_1 : Fintype ι] (𝒞 : Finset (Finset ι)), HiddenChannelCapacity.Acyclic 𝒞 ↔ ∀ (fam : List (HiddenChannelCapacity.LocalStruct ι fun x => ZMod 2)), List.map (fun x => HiddenChannelCapacity.LocalStruct.team x) fam = Finset.toList 𝒞 → HiddenChannelCapacity.Consistent fam → (∀ L ∈ fam, Finset.Nonempty (HiddenChannelCapacity.LocalStruct.rel L)) → HiddenChannelCapacity.Glues fam

title: Eidometry.Thesis.covering_dichotomy
